spec_match.rationale: Touches core terms from the chromestatus summary but skips computed-value, font-variant-emoji, high-contrast — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Some scripted state (eventListener) but no user-driven control flow — borderline static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5861138256494592 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on emoji-demo
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. introduces computed-value, font-variant-emoji, high-contrast but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-color-adjust-1/#forced-colors-properties

openQuestions.title: Add a user-driven control to emoji-demo
openQuestions.detail: Scripted page state exists but the user can't drive the change. Add at least one button / range / select that invokes the API in-page, and re-render the result so the reader can see input → output.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on emoji-demo
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: emoji-demo covers the happy path of Enable monochrome emoji rendering in forced colors mode. and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
